candidate_primitive.name: recursive/cache-oblivious or Strassen-style GEMM
candidate_primitive.contract_risk: changes the floating-point addition tree for each output cell

contract_findings.finding: matmul_2d must match textbook i-j-k ascending contracted-index accumulation bit-for-bit
the bit contract explicitly covers -0.0, +0.0, infinities, and NaN payload surface via to_bits comparisons
large and packed-B matmul paths are also pinned to textbook ascending-k to_bits equality
Dot/DotGeneral real accumulation is sequential sum += left * right over the supplied contracted order
rank-2 and batched F64 dot fast paths delegate to matmul_2d and document bit-exact ascending-k behavior
dot_general rank-2 matrix multiply asserts output bits equal a row-major i-j-k reference
einsum matmul fast path is admitted only because it preserves ascending contracted-index order bit-for-bit

contract_decision.rng_tie_ordering_surface: no RNG or tie-breaking surface in direct matmul
contract_decision.reason: no explicit large-Dot/matmul tolerance fixture currently overrides the direct bit-exact contract

next_route.primitive: KC-major packed-B layout for the blocked GEMM path
next_route.why: communication-avoiding packed-panel layout can reduce memory traffic while preserving per-output ascending-k accumulation
